TransL2E 1.0.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • TransL2E_Demo

    Transfer Learning for Robust L2E Sparse Regression

    TL_L2E_sparse.Rd

    TL_L2E_sparse performs robust transfer learning by detecting informative source datasets, co-learning on fused data, and optionally applying a debiasing step.

    TL_L2E_sparse(
  y_t,
  X_t,
  source_list,
  nfolds = 5,
  penalty = "lasso",
  max_iter = 100,
  tol = 1e-04,
  core = 1,
  seed = 123,
  debias = TRUE,
  lambda_detect = "lambda.1se",
  lambda_colearn = "lambda.1se",
  lambda_debias = "lambda.min"
)

    Arguments

    y_t

    Response vector of the target dataset.

    X_t

    Design matrix of the target dataset.

    source_list

    A list of lists, where each element contains y (response) and x (design matrix) for a source dataset.

    nfolds

    Number of cross-validation folds. Default is 5.

    penalty

    Penalty for sparsity (e.g., "lasso", "MCP", "SCAD").

    max_iter

    Maximum iterations for the optimization. Default is 100.

    tol

    Relative tolerance for convergence. Default is 1e-04.

    core

    Number of CPU cores for parallel computing during source detection.

    seed

    Seed for reproducibility. Default is 123.

    debias

    Logical; if TRUE, performs a debiasing step on the target residuals.

    lambda_detect

    Character; which lambda to use for source detection ("lambda.1se" or "lambda.min").

    lambda_colearn

    Character; which lambda to use for co-learning ("lambda.1se" or "lambda.min").

    lambda_debias

    Character; which lambda to use for debiasing ("lambda.1se" or "lambda.min").

    Value

    A list containing:

    • Coefficients: The final estimated beta coefficients.

    • Selected_Source: A list of indices and data selected from each source.

    Details

    This function implements a three-step transfer learning workflow:

    1. Source Detection: Uses parallel processing to evaluate each source dataset's similarity to the target using Hellinger distance and density-ratio weights.

    2. Co-learning: Aggregates the target data with selected source data to refine the sparse regression estimates.

    3. Debiasing: If enabled, fits a sparse L2E model on the residuals to correct for transfer bias.

    Examples

    
set.seed(123)
sigma_snr = 1

p = 50 # parameters
n_t = 200 # target sample size
n_s = 400 # source sample size


K = 2 # number of source
rho = 0.5 # level of covariate shift
Sigma = source_cov(p, rho)

beta_t = matrix(0, nrow = p)
beta_t[1:(0.1*p), 1] = 1 # 10% sparsity

sigma_ms = 0.2 # model shift level
p_s = 0.3*p

out_prop = 0.2
out_sig = 2


# simulate the target dataset
X_t = matrix(rnorm(n_t * p), nrow = n_t, ncol = p) # target covariates
y_t = X_t %*% beta_t + rnorm(n_t, mean = 0, sd = sigma_snr) # target response
    
    
# 10% outler to the samples
y_t[1:(n_t * 0.1)] = y_t[1:(n_t * 0.1)] + out_sig*max(y_t)

source_data = source_cre(n_s = n_s, p = p, p_s = p_s, 
                      snr = sigma_snr, sigma_s = sigma_ms, Sigma_S = Sigma,
                      K = K, out_prop = out_prop, out_sig = out_sig, beta_t = beta_t)
                      
                      
TL_with_debias = TL_L2E_sparse(y_t, X_t, source_list = source_data,
                              debias = TRUE, lambda_detect = "lambda.1se",
                              lambda_colearn="lambda.1se", lambda_debias="lambda.1se")
#> Step 1: Source Detection
#> Warning: already exporting variable(s): y_t, X_t, source_list, n_t, penalty, max_iter, tol, nfolds, lambda_detect
#> Step 2: Co-learning
#> Step 3: Debiasing



# the fitted error
sum((beta_t - TL_with_debias$Coefficients)^2)
#> [1] 0.3220908

    Contents
